blocks.heading: Presentation software helps you make slides. 💻
blocks.html:
Slides are like digital pages you show on a screen.

You use words, pictures and sound to share ideas.

blocks.heading: The layout is how things are placed on a slide. 📐
blocks.html:
A title might go at the top.

A picture might go in the middle.

Good layout makes a slide easy to read.

blocks.heading: A template is a ready-made design. 🎨
blocks.html:
It already has colours, fonts and a layout.

You just add your own words and pictures.

blocks.heading: A transition is how one slide changes to the next. 🔄
blocks.html:
Example: a slide might fade in, or slide across the screen.

Transitions should be simple, not distracting.

blocks.heading: Animation makes things move on a slide. ✨
blocks.html:
Example: text can fly in, or a picture can grow bigger.

Use animation only when it helps explain your idea.

blocks.heading: A hyperlink takes you to another place when you click it. 🔗
blocks.html:
It can jump to another slide, a website, or a file.

Hyperlinks are often shown in a different colour.

blocks.title: Match each word to its meaning. 🧩

pairs.definition: How things are placed on a slide 📐
A ready-made design you can use 🎨
How one slide changes to the next 🔄
Movement added to text or pictures ✨
A link you click to jump somewhere else 🔗

question.explanation: A template is a ready-made design. It already has colours and a layout for you to use.

options: Changes one slide to the next
question.explanation: A transition is the way one slide changes into the next slide, like a fade or a slide-across effect.

question.text: What happens when you click a hyperlink? 🔗
options: It jumps you to another place
question.explanation: A hyperlink takes you to another slide, a website, or a file when you click it.

blocks.heading: Bullets list ideas one at a time. •
blocks.html:
Each bullet point is one short idea.

Bullets stop a slide from having too many words.

blocks.heading: Graphics and clip art are pictures on a slide. 🖼️
blocks.html:
They help explain your idea without using words.

Clip art is a simple, ready-made picture.

blocks.heading: Print preview shows what your slides will look like on paper. 🖨️
blocks.html:
Check it before you print.

You can choose to print one slide per page, or many slides per page.

blocks.heading: Document creation means making a new presentation file. 📄
blocks.html:
You give it a name and choose where to save it.

Then you can start adding slides.

blocks.heading: Always proofread your slides before you present. 🔍
blocks.html:
Check for spelling and typing mistakes.

Proofreading means reading your work carefully to find errors.

blocks.title: Put these steps in the order you would do them when making a presentation. 📋

question.text: Why do we use bullet points? •
options: To list short ideas one at a time
question.explanation: Bullet points list short ideas one at a time. This stops a slide from having too many words.

question.text: What does print preview show you? 🖨️
options: What your slides will look like on paper
question.explanation: Print preview shows what your slides will look like before you print them.

options: Reading your work carefully to find errors
question.explanation: Proofreading means reading your work carefully to find spelling and typing mistakes.

blocks.heading: A font is the style of your letters. 🔤
blocks.html:
Some fonts look plain. Some fonts look fancy.

Pick a font that is easy to read.

blocks.heading: Font size is how big or small the letters are. 🔠
blocks.html:
Titles should use a bigger size.

Body text should use a smaller size, but still easy to read.

blocks.heading: Alignment is where text sits on the slide. ↔️
blocks.html:
Text can be left, centre, or right aligned.

Keep your alignment the same on every slide.

blocks.heading: Format means things like bold, italic, or underline. 🔡
blocks.html:
Bold makes text stand out.

Italic can show a special word.

blocks.heading: Spacing is the gap between lines and letters. ↕️
blocks.html:
Too little spacing is hard to read.

Good spacing makes your slide look neat.

blocks.heading: Target audience means the people you are presenting to. 🎯
blocks.html:
Example: young kids, adults, or experts.

You should change your words and pictures to suit them.

blocks.title: Fill in the missing word. Spacing, size, alignment and format are all features of ___.
blocks.explanation: Size, alignment, format and spacing are all features of fonts. They change how your text looks.

options: How big or small the letters are
question.explanation: Font size is how big or small the letters are. Titles usually use a bigger size.

options: The people you are presenting to
question.explanation: Your target audience is the group of people you are presenting to, like young kids or adults.

question.text: Why should you think about your target audience? 🎯
options: So you can add more transitions
So you can choose words and pictures that suit them
So you can make the file bigger
question.explanation: Thinking about your target audience helps you choose the right words and pictures so they understand you.

blocks.heading: The elements of design are the basic building blocks of a slide. 🧱
blocks.html:
They are line, shape, space, and colour.

Every good slide uses these well.

blocks.heading: Line is a mark that can divide or connect parts of a slide. ➖
blocks.html:
A line can separate a title from the body text.

Lines can also guide the eye across the slide.

blocks.heading: Shape means circles, squares, and other forms. 🔵
blocks.html:
Shapes can hold text, or act as buttons.

Shapes can also group similar ideas together.

blocks.heading: Space is the empty area on a slide. ⬜
blocks.html:
Empty space gives your eyes a rest.

Too many words and pictures with no space looks messy.

blocks.heading: Colour can highlight ideas or set a mood. 🌈
blocks.html:
Bright colours can grab attention.

Soft colours can feel calm. Always make text easy to read against the background.

blocks.heading: Balance is the principle of spreading items evenly. ⚖️
blocks.html:
A balanced slide does not feel heavy on one side.

Balance makes a slide feel comfortable to look at.

blocks.heading: Emphasis is the principle of making one thing stand out. ⭐
blocks.html:
You can use size, colour or bold text to add emphasis.

Emphasis helps your audience know what is most important.

blocks.title: Match each design word to its meaning. 🧩

pairs.definition: A mark that divides or connects parts of a slide ➖
The empty area on a slide ⬜
Highlights ideas or sets a mood 🌈
Spreading items evenly on a slide ⚖️

options: The empty area on a slide
question.explanation: Space is the empty area on a slide. It gives your eyes a rest and stops the slide looking messy.
Emphasis is making one thing stand out, often using size, colour, or bold text.

options: Spreading items evenly on a slide
question.explanation: Balance means spreading items evenly so the slide does not feel heavy on one side.
